text:
Summarize (Orchestrating the Discussion)

Discussing Solutions and Strategies

Have a class discussion about the survey data in the Initial Challenge before having students move on to the What If...? questions. The sharing, wonderings, and analyzing of the class strategies for answering the survey questions may help students make sense of and critique the What If...? questions.

Teaching Aid 4.1: Keeya's Tape Diagram from Problem 4.1 can be used to show a visual of the survey data that is in the Student Edition. Note that the numbers in the survey data are set up to make students analyze the relationships. The tacos for lunch results are both 136, with different numbers of students responding to the survey. The favorite class results both have the same number of students responding to the survey, with different number of students saying "yes." The dress code results will require students to both scale up and scale down.

Many students may use equivalent ratios to answer the questions. As questions about percents to help students see the connections in the thinking .
